description: Build a full admin portal at /admin for krystalballz.xyz. Includes simple env-var-based authentication (not next-auth), a dashboard showing key metrics, views into kbz_chron scrape data (companies, health, manual scrape trigger), subscriber management (list, export CSV, delete), and admin navigation. The portal uses the existing retro/pixel theme (Press Start 2P font) established in the admin login page.

context.existingApp: Next.js 15 with App Router, Tailwind CSS, shadcn/ui, Drizzle ORM on Vercel Postgres
context.adminPages: app/admin/page.tsx (styled login form, auth not wired), app/admin/layout.tsx (Press Start 2P font)

endpoints.GET /search: Search companies (params: q, company_name, sector, series, investor, days, limit)

kbzChron.database: MongoDB Atlas, companies DB, funded_companies collection
kbzChron.fields: company_name, funding_amount, series, sector, investors, date, source, title, url, content, description, founded_year, total_funding, valuation, created_at, enriched_at, scraped_at

subscribers.table: Subscriber in Vercel Postgres (id, email, agreedToTos, createdAt)

auth.strategy: Simple env var check against ADMIN_USERNAME and ADMIN_PASSWORD. NOT next-auth.
auth.session: HTTP-only cookie with signed JWT token scoped to /admin path

context.styling: Retro pixel theme with Press Start 2P font, dark backgrounds (#141016, #171217), gold accents (#d4a853), pixelated borders

userStories.title: Admin auth API route (login)
userStories.description: As an admin, I want a POST /api/admin/auth endpoint that validates my credentials against ADMIN_USERNAME and ADMIN_PASSWORD env vars so that I can authenticate securely.
acceptanceCriteria: Create app/api/admin/auth/route.ts with a POST handler
Accept JSON body with { username, password }
Compare against process.env.ADMIN_USERNAME and process.env.ADMIN_PASSWORD
Return 401 with { error: 'Invalid credentials' } if mismatch
Return 400 if username or password missing
On success, set an HTTP-only cookie named 'admin-session' with a signed JWT value (use jose library for JWT signing with ADMIN_SESSION_SECRET env var)
Cookie should have: httpOnly: true, secure: true, sameSite: 'lax', path: '/admin', maxAge: 86400 (24h)
Return 200 with { success: true } on valid credentials
Create lib/admin/session.ts utility with createSessionToken(payload) and verifySessionToken(token) functions

userStories.title: Admin auth verification utility
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a reusable function to verify the admin session cookie so that all admin routes can check authentication consistently.
acceptanceCriteria: In lib/admin/session.ts, export a verifyAdminSession() function that reads the 'admin-session' cookie from next/headers
Returns { authenticated: true } if valid, { authenticated: false } otherwise
Handles missing cookie, expired token, and invalid signature gracefully
Export a helper getAdminSessionFromRequest(request: Request) for use in API routes
Ensure the module compiles with npx tsc --noEmit

userStories.title: Wire up admin login form to auth API
userStories.description: As an admin, I want the existing login form at /admin to actually call the auth API and redirect me to /admin/dashboard on success so that I can log in.
acceptanceCriteria: Modify app/admin/page.tsx handleSubmit to POST to /api/admin/auth with { username, password }
Show loading state on the ENTER button during submission (text changes to 'LOADING...' and button is disabled)
On 200 response, redirect to /admin/dashboard using router.push
On 401 response, show error text 'INVALID CREDENTIALS' in red below the form
On network error, show 'CONNECTION ERROR' message
Clear error message when user starts typing again

userStories.title: Admin route protection middleware
userStories.description: As a system, I need all /admin/* routes (except /admin login page) to require authentication so unauthorized users cannot access admin features.
acceptanceCriteria: Use Next.js middleware.ts at project root to intercept /admin/* requests
If no valid admin-session cookie, redirect to /admin (login page) for all /admin/* paths except /admin itself and /api/admin/auth
If the project already has a middleware.ts, add the admin check as an additional matcher without breaking existing middleware
If no middleware.ts exists, create one with matcher for /admin/:path+ patterns
The /admin page (login) and /api/admin/auth must remain accessible without a session

userStories.title: Admin logout functionality
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to log out so that my session is invalidated and I'm returned to the login page.
acceptanceCriteria: Create POST /api/admin/auth/logout route in app/api/admin/auth/logout/route.ts
Clear the admin-session cookie by setting it with maxAge: 0
Return 200 with { success: true }
The logout action will be called from a button in the admin nav (built in a later story)

userStories.title: Admin layout with sidebar navigation
userStories.description: As an admin, I want a persistent sidebar navigation on all admin pages (post-login) so I can navigate between dashboard, companies, subscribers, and settings.
acceptanceCriteria: Create app/admin/(portal)/layout.tsx as a layout for authenticated admin pages
Layout includes a left sidebar with navigation links: Dashboard, Companies, Subscribers, Scrape Status, Settings
Sidebar uses the retro pixel theme consistent with the login page (dark bg, gold accents, Press Start 2P font)
Sidebar shows 'KRYSTAL BALL Z' branding at top and a LOGOUT button at bottom
LOGOUT button calls POST /api/admin/auth/logout then redirects to /admin
Active route is visually highlighted in the sidebar
Sidebar is collapsible on mobile (hamburger menu)
Main content area takes remaining width with proper padding

userStories.title: kbz_chron proxy API route - search companies
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a server-side proxy to the kbz_chron /search endpoint so that the admin UI can fetch company data without exposing the Fly.io URL to the client.
acceptanceCriteria: Create app/api/admin/companies/route.ts with a GET handler
Verify admin session before processing (return 401 if not authenticated)
Forward query parameters (q, company_name, sector, series, investor, days, limit) to KBZ_CHRON_URL/search
Return the JSON response from kbz_chron to the client
Handle kbz_chron errors gracefully (return 502 with error message if upstream fails)
Set a 10-second timeout on the fetch to kbz_chron
Use KBZ_CHRON_URL env var (default: https://kbz-chron.fly.dev)

userStories.title: kbz_chron proxy API route - health and status
userStories.description: As a developer, I need proxy endpoints for kbz_chron health and status so the admin dashboard can display scrape service health.
acceptanceCriteria: Create app/api/admin/scrape/health/route.ts - proxies GET to kbz_chron /health
Create app/api/admin/scrape/status/route.ts - proxies GET to kbz_chron / (root, returns last_run info)
Both routes verify admin session (return 401 if unauthenticated)
Both handle upstream errors gracefully with 502 responses
Both use the KBZ_CHRON_URL env var

userStories.title: Admin dashboard overview page
userStories.description: As an admin, I want a dashboard at /admin/dashboard showing key metrics at a glance so I can quickly assess system health.
acceptanceCriteria: Create app/admin/(portal)/dashboard/page.tsx
Display metric cards: Total Subscribers (from /api/subscribe GET), Scrape Service Health (from /api/admin/scrape/health), Last Scrape Time (from /api/admin/scrape/status)
Each card shows a label, value, and status indicator (green/red dot for health)
Show a 'Recent Companies' preview list (last 5 companies from /api/admin/companies?days=7&limit=5)
Use the retro pixel theme styling
Show loading skeletons while data is being fetched
Show error states if any API call fails

userStories.title: Companies list view with search and pagination
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to browse all scraped companies with search and pagination so I can find and review funding data.
acceptanceCriteria: Create app/admin/(portal)/companies/page.tsx
Display a table/list of companies with columns: Company Name, Funding Amount, Series, Sector, Source, Date
Add a search input that filters by company name (uses the q parameter on /api/admin/companies)
Add filter dropdowns for sector and series
Default to showing last 30 days of data
Implement client-side pagination with limit=20 per page (Next/Previous buttons)
Use retro pixel theme styling for the table
Show loading skeleton while fetching
Show 'No companies found' empty state
Each company name is clickable and links to /admin/companies/[name]

userStories.title: Company detail view
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to click on a company and see all its details so I can review the full funding record.
acceptanceCriteria: Create app/admin/(portal)/companies/[name]/page.tsx
Fetch company data from /api/admin/companies?company_name=[name]
Display all available fields: company_name, funding_amount, series, sector, investors, date, source, title, url (as clickable link), description, founded_year, total_funding, valuation
Show a back button that returns to the companies list
Handle case where company is not found with appropriate message
Use retro pixel theme styling with card-based layout

userStories.title: Scrape status and health dashboard
userStories.description: As an admin, I want a dedicated page showing the scrape service status, last run details, and health so I can monitor the data pipeline.
acceptanceCriteria: Create app/admin/(portal)/scrape/page.tsx
Show kbz_chron health status (green/red indicator from /api/admin/scrape/health)
Show last run timestamp and status from /api/admin/scrape/status
Show last run details if available (articles found, companies inserted/skipped)
Auto-refresh status every 30 seconds
Show a 'Trigger Scrape' button (wired in a later story, disabled for now with tooltip 'Coming soon')
Use retro pixel theme styling

userStories.title: kbz_chron proxy API route - trigger scrape
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a proxy endpoint to trigger a manual scrape so the admin can initiate data collection on demand.
acceptanceCriteria: Create app/api/admin/scrape/trigger/route.ts with a POST handler
Verify admin session (return 401 if unauthenticated)
POST to KBZ_CHRON_URL/run endpoint to trigger full scrape
Set a 120-second timeout (scraping takes time)
Return the result JSON from kbz_chron
Handle upstream errors with 502 response

userStories.title: Wire trigger scrape button on scrape status page
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to click a button to trigger a manual scrape and see the result so I can collect data on demand.
acceptanceCriteria: Enable the 'Trigger Scrape' button on app/admin/(portal)/scrape/page.tsx
On click, POST to /api/admin/scrape/trigger
Show a loading/spinner state with text 'SCRAPING...' (button disabled during operation)
On success, show a result summary (articles found, companies inserted) and refresh the status display
On error, show error message in red
Add a confirmation dialog before triggering ('Are you sure? This will scrape TechCrunch and TechStartups.')

userStories.title: Subscribers list view with search
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to view all email subscribers with search functionality so I can manage the subscriber base.
acceptanceCriteria: Create app/admin/(portal)/subscribers/page.tsx
Fetch subscribers from /api/subscribe GET endpoint
Display a table with columns: Email, Agreed to TOS, Signed Up (formatted date)
Add a search input that filters subscribers by email (client-side filter)
Show total subscriber count prominently
Sort by createdAt descending (most recent first) by default
Show loading skeleton while fetching
Show 'No subscribers yet' empty state
Use retro pixel theme styling

userStories.title: Export subscribers as CSV
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to download all subscribers as a CSV file so I can use the data in external tools.
acceptanceCriteria: Add an 'EXPORT CSV' button to the subscribers page
Create app/api/admin/subscribers/export/route.ts that returns subscribers as CSV
CSV includes columns: email, agreedToTos, createdAt
Response has Content-Type: text/csv and Content-Disposition: attachment; filename=subscribers-YYYY-MM-DD.csv
Verify admin session (return 401 if unauthenticated)
Button triggers a download via window.location or anchor tag href

userStories.title: Delete subscriber capability
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to delete a subscriber so I can manage the subscriber list (e.g., remove test entries or honor removal requests).
acceptanceCriteria: Create app/api/admin/subscribers/[id]/route.ts with a DELETE handler
Verify admin session (return 401 if unauthenticated)
Delete the subscriber record from Postgres by id
Return 200 with { success: true } on success
Return 404 if subscriber not found
Add deleteSubscriber({ id }) function to lib/db/queries.ts
Add a delete button (trash icon or 'X') next to each subscriber row on the subscribers page
Show a confirmation dialog before deleting
After deletion, refresh the subscriber list

userStories.title: Subscriber stats and signup chart
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to see subscriber signup trends over time so I can understand growth patterns.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a stats section at the top of app/admin/(portal)/subscribers/page.tsx
Show: Total Subscribers, Signups This Week, Signups This Month
Create app/api/admin/subscribers/stats/route.ts that returns { total, thisWeek, thisMonth }
Verify admin session in the API route
Add getSubscriberStats() function to lib/db/queries.ts using count() with date filters
Display a simple bar chart of signups by day for the last 14 days (inline SVG or CSS bars, no external chart library)
Use retro pixel theme styling

userStories.title: Admin settings page
userStories.description: As an admin, I want a settings page showing system configuration status so I can verify the deployment is properly configured.
acceptanceCriteria: Create app/admin/(portal)/settings/page.tsx
Display environment variable status (present/missing, NOT the values): ADMIN_USERNAME, ADMIN_PASSWORD, ADMIN_SESSION_SECRET, KBZ_CHRON_URL, POSTGRES_URL
Create app/api/admin/settings/route.ts that returns env var presence checks (boolean for each)
Verify admin session in the API route
Show kbz_chron service URL (masked) and connection status
Show database connection status (simple check via a count query)
Use retro pixel theme with a config-panel aesthetic

userStories.title: Error handling and loading states polish
userStories.description: As an admin, I want consistent error handling and loading states across all admin pages so the portal feels polished and reliable.
acceptanceCriteria: Create a reusable AdminLoadingSkeleton component at components/admin/loading-skeleton.tsx with the retro theme
Create a reusable AdminErrorState component at components/admin/error-state.tsx with retry button
Create a reusable AdminEmptyState component at components/admin/empty-state.tsx
Apply these components to all admin pages that don't already use them: dashboard, companies, subscribers, scrape, settings
Ensure all fetch calls have try/catch with proper error display
Add toast notifications (using sonner, already installed) for success/error on actions (delete subscriber, trigger scrape, logout)
Verify all pages handle the unauthenticated redirect gracefully (no flash of content)

existingTables.Subscriber: id (uuid PK), email (varchar 256 unique), agreedToTos (boolean), createdAt (timestamp)

dataModel.noNewTables: This project does not require new database tables. Admin auth uses env vars + cookies (no DB). Company data lives in kbz_chron MongoDB (accessed via proxy). Subscribers already exist.

envVars.ADMIN_SESSION_SECRET: Secret key for signing admin session JWT (min 32 chars)
envVars.KBZ_CHRON_URL: Base URL for kbz_chron service (default: https://kbz-chron.fly.dev)

endpoints.POST /api/admin/auth: Admin login - validate credentials against env vars, set session cookie
endpoints.GET /api/admin/companies: Proxy to kbz_chron /search with query params (q, company_name, sector, series, investor, days, limit)
endpoints.GET /api/admin/settings: Env var presence and connection status checks

outOfScope: Multi-user admin accounts (single admin via env vars)
Editing or creating company records from admin (read-only view of kbz_chron data)
Dark/light theme toggle (always dark retro theme)
Mobile-optimized admin experience (desktop-first, responsive sidebar only)
definitionOfDone: Admin can log in at /admin with env var credentials and be redirected to /admin/dashboard
Unauthenticated users are redirected to /admin login page when accessing any /admin/* route
Admin can log out and session is cleared
Dashboard shows subscriber count, scrape health, and last scrape time
Admin can browse, search, and filter companies from kbz_chron
Admin can view individual company details
Admin can monitor scrape service health and trigger manual scrapes
Admin can view, search, export (CSV), and delete subscribers
Subscriber stats show total, weekly, and monthly counts
Settings page shows env var configuration status
All pages have loading states, error handling, and empty states
All admin API routes verify the admin session cookie
All UI uses the retro pixel theme consistent with the login page
